cards.subject: ap-world-history
cards.prompt: The Jurchen chieftain who unified the tribes of Manchuria in the early 17th century, created the banner military system, and laid the foundation for the Qing Dynasty

cards.subject: ap-world-history
cards.prompt: The long-reigning Qing emperor (1661–1722) who consolidated Manchu rule over China, expanded the empire into Taiwan and Central Asia, and patronized Confucian scholarship and Jesuit science

cards.subject: ap-world-history
cards.prompt: The Qing emperor (1735–1796) who presided over the empire's greatest territorial extent, sponsored the Complete Library of the Four Treasuries, and rejected British trade overtures in the Macartney embassy

cards.subject: ap-world-history
cards.prompt: The Ming admiral who commanded seven massive treasure-fleet voyages (1405–1433) through the Indian Ocean, projecting Chinese power from Southeast Asia to East Africa before the Ming turned inward

cards.subject: ap-world-history
cards.prompt: The Italian Jesuit missionary who arrived at the Ming court in the late 16th century, mastered Chinese and Confucian texts, and introduced European cartography, astronomy, and mathematics to China

cards.subject: ap-world-history
cards.prompt: The ethnically Han Chinese imperial dynasty (1368–1644) that expelled the Mongols, restored the civil service examination, rebuilt the Great Wall, and dispatched the Zheng He voyages

cards.subject: ap-world-history
cards.prompt: The vast walled palace complex at the heart of Beijing built under the Yongle Emperor as the ceremonial and political seat of Ming and Qing rulers, off-limits to commoners for centuries

cards.subject: ap-world-history
cards.prompt: The third Ming emperor (r. 1402–1424) who moved the capital to Beijing, commissioned the Forbidden City, sponsored Zheng He's voyages, and ordered the compilation of a vast encyclopedia

cards.subject: ap-world-history
cards.prompt: Castrated male servants who managed the inner palace, controlled access to the emperor, and often wielded enormous political power within the Ming and Qing courts

cards.subject: ap-world-history
cards.prompt: The semi-nomadic Tungusic people of Manchuria, unified by Nurhaci in the early 17th century, who later rebranded themselves as the Manchu and conquered Ming China

cards.subject: ap-world-history
cards.prompt: The ethnic group descended from the Jurchen who founded the Qing Dynasty in 1644 and ruled China as a conquest elite, maintaining a separate banner social system

cards.subject: ap-world-history
cards.prompt: The final Chinese imperial dynasty (1644–1912), founded by the Manchu, that greatly expanded China's frontiers into Inner Asia while preserving Confucian bureaucracy

cards.subject: ap-world-history
cards.prompt: The rigorous Confucian-based testing system used to recruit scholar-officials into the Chinese bureaucracy, restored and expanded under the Ming and Qing to sustain imperial administration

cards.subject: ap-world-history
cards.prompt: The Chinese practice, widespread among gentry families through the Qing era, of tightly wrapping young girls' feet to keep them small as a marker of status and restricted mobility

cards.subject: ap-world-history
cards.prompt: The Confucian virtue of deep respect, loyalty, and obedience owed by children to parents and ancestors, foundational to Ming and Qing family life and social order

cards.subject: ap-world-history
cards.prompt: The long braided hairstyle with a shaved forehead that Qing authorities required all Han Chinese men to wear as a visible sign of submission to Manchu rule

cards.subject: ap-world-history
cards.prompt: A confidential communication system used by Qing emperors such as Kangxi and Yongzheng that allowed trusted officials to bypass the regular bureaucracy and report directly to the throne

cards.subject: ap-world-history
cards.prompt: A massive early-15th-century compendium of Chinese knowledge commissioned by the Yongle Emperor, drawing on thousands of works across classics, history, philosophy, and the arts

cards.subject: ap-world-history
cards.prompt: An early-18th-century Qing imperial encyclopedia compiled under the Kangxi and Yongzheng emperors, organizing Chinese knowledge into thousands of categories

cards.subject: ap-world-history
cards.prompt: A monumental Qing literary project commissioned by the Qianlong Emperor that gathered and censored thousands of Chinese works across four divisions of classical knowledge
cards.answer: Complete Library of the Four Treasuries

cards.subject: ap-world-history
cards.prompt: The Manchu-organized hereditary military units, divided into color-coded banners, that formed the backbone of Qing military power and occupied garrisons across the empire

cards.subject: ap-world-history
cards.prompt: The title adopted by Russian rulers beginning with Ivan III and Ivan IV, derived from 'Caesar' and asserting autocratic authority as the legitimate successors of the Byzantine emperors

cards.subject: ap-world-history
cards.prompt: The Russian imperial dynasty that took the throne in 1613 after the Time of Troubles and ruled until the 1917 revolution, modernizing and expanding the empire

cards.subject: ap-world-history
cards.prompt: The hereditary landed aristocracy of Russia whose influence Ivan IV and later Peter the Great worked to break in favor of a centralized autocracy

cards.subject: ap-world-history
cards.prompt: The legal code issued under Ivan III and expanded under Ivan IV that standardized Muscovite law, strengthened the power of the tsar, and tightened restrictions on peasant movement

cards.subject: ap-world-history
cards.prompt: An assembly of the realm convened by Russian tsars that brought together clergy, nobles, and townsmen to advise on taxation, war, and succession, notably electing the first Romanov in 1613

cards.subject: ap-world-history
cards.prompt: The brightly colored onion-domed cathedral on Red Square commissioned by Ivan the Terrible to commemorate the Russian conquest of Kazan, an icon of Muscovite Orthodox architecture

cards.subject: ap-world-history
cards.prompt: The separate territory and secret-police apparatus created by Ivan the Terrible to terrorize and dispossess the boyar nobility, leaving large portions of Russia devastated

cards.subject: ap-world-history
cards.prompt: The hereditary Muscovite musketeer corps whose rebellions Peter the Great brutally suppressed as part of his campaign to modernize the Russian military

cards.subject: ap-world-history
cards.prompt: The vast subarctic region east of the Urals colonized by Russian traders, Cossacks, and exiles beginning in the late 16th century, providing furs, minerals, and political banishment sites

cards.subject: ap-world-history
cards.prompt: The deliberate adoption of Western European models of administration, technology, dress, and culture, pursued aggressively by Peter the Great to transform Russia into a modern power

cards.subject: ap-world-history
cards.prompt: The broader process of adopting industrial, scientific, and bureaucratic reforms drawn from Western Europe, pursued by Russian rulers from Peter the Great through Catherine the Great

cards.subject: ap-world-history
cards.prompt: The new Baltic-coast capital founded by Peter the Great in 1703 as Russia's 'window to the West,' built on conquered Swedish territory as a planned European-style city

cards.subject: ap-world-history
cards.prompt: The elaborate seaside palace complex built for Peter the Great outside St. Petersburg, modeled on Versailles and showcasing Russia's new Westernized grandeur

cards.subject: ap-world-history
cards.prompt: The baroque imperial residence in St. Petersburg, greatly expanded under Catherine the Great, that served as the official home of the Romanovs until 1917

cards.subject: ap-world-history
cards.prompt: A massive Cossack and peasant uprising against serfdom and noble privilege in the 1770s, led by a pretender claiming to be Peter III, crushed by Catherine the Great

cards.subject: ap-world-history
cards.prompt: The 15th-century Muscovite ruler who ended Mongol tributary rule, tripled Russia's territory, adopted the title of tsar, and claimed Moscow as the 'Third Rome'

cards.subject: ap-world-history
cards.prompt: The first formally crowned tsar of Russia, known for his expansion into Kazan and Siberia, creation of the oprichnina, and violent campaigns against the boyar nobility

cards.subject: ap-world-history
cards.prompt: The Romanov tsar (r. 1682–1725) who Westernized Russian government, dress, and military, built St. Petersburg as a new capital, and defeated Sweden to secure a Baltic coastline

cards.subject: ap-world-history
cards.prompt: The German-born Russian empress (r. 1762–1796) who expanded the empire into Poland and the Black Sea, patronized Enlightenment ideas, and tightened serfdom after the Pugachev Rebellion

cards.subject: ap-world-history
cards.prompt: The late-13th-century Turkish ghazi leader in Anatolia whose followers founded the Ottoman dynasty, taking their name from his and launching centuries of expansion

cards.subject: ap-world-history
cards.prompt: The Ottoman sultan who captured Constantinople in 1453, ending the Byzantine Empire, and transformed the city into the new Ottoman capital and center of Islamic learning

cards.subject: ap-world-history
cards.prompt: The Ottoman sultan (r. 1520–1566) known in the West as 'the Magnificent' and at home as 'the Lawgiver,' who expanded the empire into Hungary, codified Ottoman law, and patronized a golden age of arts

cards.subject: ap-world-history
cards.prompt: The 1453 Ottoman siege and capture of the Byzantine capital by Mehmet II, ending over a millennium of Roman rule and opening the city as the new Ottoman center of power

cards.subject: ap-world-history
cards.prompt: The Ottoman system of levying Christian boys from the Balkans, converting them to Islam, and training them for service as janissaries or palace administrators

cards.subject: ap-world-history
cards.prompt: The elite Ottoman infantry corps recruited through the devshirme system, equipped with firearms and owing allegiance directly to the sultan

cards.subject: ap-world-history
cards.prompt: Semi-autonomous religious communities within the Ottoman Empire, led by their own clergy, that governed the internal affairs of Christian and Jewish subjects

cards.subject: ap-world-history
cards.prompt: The head tax paid by non-Muslim subjects (dhimmis) in exchange for protection and religious tolerance within Islamic empires such as the Ottoman and Mughal

cards.subject: ap-world-history
cards.prompt: The Ottoman practice of auctioning the right to collect taxes in a district to private contractors, who kept any surplus beyond the fixed payment owed to the state

cards.subject: ap-world-history
cards.prompt: The inner palace school at Topkapi where young devshirme recruits received rigorous training in languages, Islamic scholarship, and administration before elite Ottoman service

cards.subject: ap-world-history
cards.prompt: The large-caliber gunpowder artillery the Ottomans deployed decisively at sieges such as Constantinople in 1453, a hallmark of the 'gunpowder empires'

cards.subject: ap-world-history
cards.prompt: The monumental Byzantine cathedral in Constantinople that Mehmet II converted into a mosque after the 1453 conquest, symbolizing Ottoman succession to Roman imperial prestige

cards.subject: ap-world-history
cards.prompt: The chief minister of the Ottoman sultan, who presided over the imperial council and wielded broad authority over administration, finance, and the military

cards.subject: ap-world-history
cards.prompt: The 1683 siege in which a Polish-led coalition relieved the Habsburg capital and decisively halted Ottoman expansion into central Europe

cards.subject: ap-world-history
cards.prompt: The sprawling sultanic palace complex overlooking the Bosphorus in Istanbul, the political and ceremonial heart of the Ottoman Empire for nearly four centuries

cards.subject: ap-world-history
cards.prompt: The great imperial mosque in Istanbul commissioned by Suleiman I and designed by the architect Sinan, a landmark of classical Ottoman architecture

cards.subject: ap-world-history
cards.prompt: The Ottoman legal framework regulating the status, obligations, and taxation of non-Muslim peasant subjects under imperial rule

cards.subject: ap-world-history
cards.prompt: Finely detailed painted illustrations produced in Ottoman court workshops that depicted historical events, court ceremonies, and sultanic portraits in a distinctive Islamic style

cards.subject: ap-world-history
cards.prompt: The branch of Shi'a Islam that Shah Ismail I imposed as the state religion of Safavid Persia, distinguishing the empire sharply from its Sunni Ottoman and Mughal neighbors

cards.subject: ap-world-history
cards.prompt: The Turkic Shi'a tribal warriors, known as 'redheads' for their distinctive caps, who formed the original military backbone of the Safavid Empire under Shah Ismail I

cards.subject: ap-world-history
cards.prompt: The 1514 engagement in which Ottoman gunpowder forces crushed the Safavid cavalry under Shah Ismail I, establishing a long-term frontier between the two empires

cards.subject: ap-world-history
cards.prompt: The grand imperial plaza built by Shah Abbas I in Isfahan, flanked by mosques, a royal palace, and a bazaar, showcasing Safavid architecture and urban planning

cards.subject: ap-world-history
cards.prompt: The late-14th-century Turco-Mongol conqueror from Central Asia whose brutal campaigns devastated much of the Islamic world and whose descendants founded the Mughal dynasty

cards.subject: ap-world-history
cards.prompt: The founder of the Safavid Empire in 1501 who imposed Twelver Shi'a Islam as the state religion of Persia and led the Qizilbash cavalry

cards.subject: ap-world-history
cards.prompt: The most powerful Safavid ruler (r. 1588–1629) who reorganized the military with gunpowder and slave soldiers, moved the capital to Isfahan, and presided over the empire's cultural peak

cards.subject: ap-world-history
cards.prompt: The Central Asian descendant of Tamerlane and Genghis Khan who invaded northern India, defeated the Delhi Sultanate at Panipat in 1526, and founded the Mughal Empire

cards.subject: ap-world-history
cards.prompt: The third Mughal emperor (r. 1556–1605) who expanded the empire across most of northern India, abolished the jizya on non-Muslims, and promoted a syncretic religious policy

cards.subject: ap-world-history
cards.prompt: The 15th–16th-century founder of Sikhism who preached a monotheistic faith blending elements of Hindu and Islamic devotion and rejecting caste distinctions

cards.subject: ap-world-history
cards.prompt: The fourth Mughal emperor (r. 1605–1627), son of Akbar, who continued his father's pluralistic policies and patronized a flourishing school of Mughal miniature painting

cards.subject: ap-world-history
cards.prompt: The fifth Mughal emperor (r. 1628–1658) who built the Taj Mahal as a tomb for his wife Mumtaz Mahal and oversaw an age of monumental architecture in Delhi and Agra

cards.subject: ap-world-history
cards.prompt: The sixth Mughal emperor (r. 1658–1707) who expanded the empire to its greatest extent but strained it with constant warfare and reimposed the jizya, provoking Hindu and Sikh resistance

cards.subject: ap-world-history
cards.prompt: The 1526 engagement in which Babur's outnumbered forces used artillery and cavalry tactics to crush the Delhi Sultanate, opening northern India to Mughal rule

cards.subject: ap-world-history
cards.prompt: Akbar the Great's short-lived syncretic religious order that combined elements of Islam, Hinduism, Zoroastrianism, and Christianity in an effort to unify his diverse subjects

cards.subject: ap-world-history
cards.prompt: The blending of beliefs, practices, or cultural elements from different traditions — evident in Akbar's Din-i-Ilahi and in Sikhism's fusion of Hindu and Islamic devotion

cards.subject: ap-world-history
cards.prompt: The monotheistic religion founded by Guru Nanak in the Punjab that rejects caste distinctions and emphasizes devotion to one formless God, the teachings of ten gurus, and community service

cards.subject: ap-world-history
cards.prompt: The gold-plated Sikh gurdwara at Amritsar, the holiest shrine of Sikhism, constructed under the fifth guru in the Punjab during the Mughal era

cards.subject: ap-world-history
cards.prompt: The fertile river-plain region in northwest South Asia, the cultural heartland of Sikhism and a frequently contested frontier between Mughal, Persian, and later British powers

cards.subject: ap-world-history
cards.prompt: The white marble mausoleum in Agra commissioned by Shah Jahan for his wife Mumtaz Mahal, a masterpiece of Mughal architecture blending Persian, Islamic, and Indian styles

cards.subject: ap-world-history
cards.prompt: The massive red sandstone citadel in Delhi built by Shah Jahan as the Mughal imperial residence, its walled complex housing palaces, gardens, and audience halls

cards.subject: ap-world-history
cards.prompt: A vividly tiled 17th-century mosque in Lahore commissioned during Shah Jahan's reign, celebrated for its elaborate fresco and mosaic decoration

cards.subject: ap-world-history
cards.prompt: A 17th-century Mughal mosque in Thatta (modern Pakistan) commissioned by Shah Jahan, known for its extensive blue tilework and distinctive acoustics

cards.subject: ap-world-history
cards.prompt: The body of Islamic religious law derived from the Quran and Hadith that governed personal, family, and commercial life in Muslim empires including the Mughal under rulers such as Aurangzeb

cards.subject: ap-world-history
cards.prompt: Hereditary local landholders in Mughal India who collected taxes from peasants on behalf of the emperor in exchange for keeping a share, often becoming quasi-autonomous regional powers

cards.subject: ap-world-history
cards.prompt: The prolonged 17th–18th-century conflicts in which the Hindu Maratha Confederacy resisted and eventually rolled back Mughal power across western and central India

cards.subject: ap-world-history
cards.prompt: The capital of the Songhai Empire on the Niger River, a major Saharan trade hub and center of Islamic learning during the 15th and 16th centuries

cards.subject: ap-world-history
cards.prompt: The legendary Niger River city in Mali and later Songhai, famed for its Sankore mosque-university and as a center of Islamic scholarship and trans-Saharan trade

cards.subject: ap-world-history
cards.prompt: An ancient West African trading city on the Niger's inland delta, renowned for its massive mud-brick Great Mosque and its role in Songhai commerce and Islamic scholarship

cards.subject: ap-world-history
cards.prompt: The late-15th-century founder-conqueror of the Songhai Empire who captured Timbuktu and Jenne and built West Africa's largest empire along the Niger River

cards.subject: ap-world-history
cards.prompt: The massive shogunal fortress in Edo (modern Tokyo) that served as the political center of the Tokugawa Shogunate, surrounded by the residences of daimyo in attendance

cards.subject: ap-world-history
cards.prompt: The Tokugawa-era Japanese policy of strict seclusion, closing the country to most foreign trade and travel while permitting limited Dutch and Chinese contact at Nagasaki

cards.subject: ap-world-history
cards.prompt: The samurai class under Tokugawa peace who, with wars ended, became stipended bureaucrats and administrators dependent on rice salaries from their daimyo

cards.subject: ap-world-history
cards.prompt: The victor of the 1600 Battle of Sekigahara who became shogun in 1603 and founded the Tokugawa Shogunate, establishing more than 250 years of centralized peace in Japan

cards.subject: ap-world-history
cards.prompt: The political doctrine that monarchs derive their authority directly from God and are answerable only to God, used to justify absolutist rule in 17th-century Europe

cards.subject: ap-world-history
cards.prompt: The vast royal palace outside Paris built under Louis XIV as the seat of the French court, where nobles were kept under royal supervision in a highly ritualized setting

cards.subject: ap-world-history
cards.prompt: English Calvinists who sought to purify the Church of England of Catholic practices and who played a central role in the English Civil War under Oliver Cromwell

cards.subject: ap-world-history
cards.prompt: The English representative assembly of lords and commons whose struggle with the Stuart monarchy over taxation and religion produced the Civil War and Glorious Revolution

cards.subject: ap-world-history
cards.prompt: The 1688 overthrow of James II of England in favor of William and Mary, which established Parliamentary supremacy and a Bill of Rights limiting royal power

cards.subject: ap-world-history
cards.prompt: The chief minister of Louis XIII who centralized French royal power by curbing the Huguenots and nobility and intervening in the Thirty Years' War to weaken Habsburg rivals

cards.subject: ap-world-history
cards.prompt: The 'Sun King' (r. 1643–1715) who embodied European absolutism, built the Palace of Versailles, revoked the Edict of Nantes, and fought wars to expand France's borders

cards.subject: ap-world-history
cards.prompt: The Stuart king whose conflicts with Parliament over taxation and religion triggered the English Civil War and who was tried and executed in 1649

cards.subject: ap-world-history
cards.prompt: The Puritan general who led Parliamentary forces to victory in the English Civil War, oversaw the execution of Charles I, and ruled England as Lord Protector

cards.subject: ap-world-history
cards.prompt: The Stuart king whose 1660 Restoration ended Cromwell's Commonwealth and who navigated uneasy relations with Parliament before being succeeded by his Catholic brother James II

cards.subject: ap-world-history
cards.prompt: The joint Protestant monarchs installed by Parliament in the Glorious Revolution of 1688 who accepted the Bill of Rights and secured a constitutional monarchy in England

cards.subject: ap-world-history
cards.prompt: The Renaissance intellectual movement that revived classical Greek and Roman texts and emphasized human potential, civic virtue, and secular learning alongside Christian faith

cards.subject: ap-world-history
cards.prompt: A wealthy individual, family, or institution — such as the Medici or the papacy — that financed Renaissance artists, scholars, and architects in exchange for prestige

cards.subject: ap-world-history
cards.prompt: A formal contract under which a Renaissance patron paid an artist to produce a specific work, typically specifying subject, materials, and deadlines

cards.subject: ap-world-history
cards.prompt: The 15th-century Florentine banker who, through wealth and political influence, made the Medici family the dominant patrons of Renaissance art and learning

cards.subject: ap-world-history
cards.prompt: The grandson of Cosimo, known as 'the Magnificent,' whose late-15th-century rule in Florence marked the height of Medici cultural patronage and classical humanist learning

cards.subject: ap-world-history
cards.prompt: The quintessential Renaissance polymath whose paintings such as the Mona Lisa and Last Supper, and notebooks on anatomy and engineering, embody the era's fusion of art and science

cards.subject: ap-world-history
cards.prompt: The High Renaissance sculptor and painter whose works include the statue of David, the Pieta, and the Sistine Chapel ceiling frescoes commissioned by the papacy

cards.subject: ap-world-history
cards.prompt: The leading northern Renaissance humanist whose satirical 'In Praise of Folly' and Greek edition of the New Testament exposed church abuses and influenced the Reformation

cards.subject: ap-world-history
cards.prompt: The Spanish Golden Age author whose early-17th-century novel 'Don Quixote' satirized chivalric romances and is often considered the first modern European novel

cards.subject: ap-world-history
cards.prompt: The English playwright and poet of the late 16th and early 17th centuries whose plays — from Hamlet to Macbeth — defined Elizabethan drama and shaped the English language

cards.subject: ap-world-history
cards.prompt: The late-medieval Florentine poet whose 'Divine Comedy,' written in vernacular Italian, bridged the medieval and Renaissance worlds and became a foundation of Italian literature

cards.subject: ap-world-history
cards.prompt: The Florentine political theorist whose early-16th-century treatise 'The Prince' argued that rulers must be willing to act pragmatically and ruthlessly to maintain power

cards.subject: ap-world-history
cards.prompt: A broad category of Christians who broke from the authority of the Roman Catholic Church in the 16th century, rejecting papal supremacy and emphasizing scripture and faith

cards.subject: ap-world-history
cards.prompt: The castle church in the German town of Wittenberg on whose door Martin Luther is said to have posted the 95 Theses in 1517, sparking the Protestant Reformation

cards.subject: ap-world-history
cards.prompt: A religious belief or practice formally condemned as contrary to official Church doctrine, punishable historically by excommunication, imprisonment, or execution

cards.subject: ap-world-history
cards.prompt: Sacred rites such as baptism and the Eucharist held by Christians to convey divine grace, their number and meaning became a central point of dispute between Catholics and Protestants

cards.subject: ap-world-history
cards.prompt: A payment or act that the late-medieval Catholic Church claimed would reduce punishment in purgatory for sin, whose sale prompted Martin Luther's 95 Theses

cards.subject: ap-world-history
cards.prompt: The practice of lending money at interest, long condemned as sinful by the medieval Church but increasingly tolerated and debated during the commercial expansion of early modern Europe

cards.subject: ap-world-history
cards.prompt: Martin Luther's 1517 list of objections to the sale of indulgences and other Roman Catholic practices, widely reprinted and credited with igniting the Protestant Reformation

cards.subject: ap-world-history
cards.prompt: The Calvinist doctrine that God eternally chose in advance which souls would be saved and which would be damned, independent of human merit or action

cards.subject: ap-world-history
cards.prompt: Pertaining to the Church of England, established when King Henry VIII broke with Rome in the 1530s and consolidated under Elizabeth I as a distinctly Protestant national church

cards.subject: ap-world-history
cards.prompt: The Catholic Church's 16th-century response to the Protestant Reformation, combining doctrinal clarification at the Council of Trent with missionary efforts and the Inquisition

cards.subject: ap-world-history
cards.prompt: The 1545–1563 Catholic ecumenical council that reaffirmed core doctrines, reformed clerical abuses, and set the course of the Counter-Reformation

cards.subject: ap-world-history
cards.prompt: The disciplined Catholic religious order founded by Ignatius of Loyola in 1540 that led Counter-Reformation education and missions from Europe to East Asia and the Americas

cards.subject: ap-world-history
cards.prompt: The Catholic tribunal established in late-15th-century Spain to identify and punish heretics, particularly converted Jews and Muslims, and later Protestants

cards.subject: ap-world-history
cards.prompt: The large fleet dispatched by Philip II of Spain in 1588 to invade Protestant England, whose defeat marked a turning point in the Reformation-era balance of power

cards.subject: ap-world-history
cards.prompt: The 14th-century English theologian who attacked papal authority and indulgences and oversaw the first English translations of the Bible, anticipating the Protestant Reformation

cards.subject: ap-world-history
cards.prompt: The early-15th-century Bohemian reformer influenced by Wycliffe who denounced church corruption and was burned at the stake in 1415, inspiring the Hussite movement

cards.subject: ap-world-history
cards.prompt: The German monk whose 1517 posting of the 95 Theses launched the Protestant Reformation and whose doctrines of sola scriptura and justification by faith redefined Western Christianity

cards.subject: ap-world-history
cards.prompt: The French-born reformer based in Geneva whose teaching of predestination and strict moral discipline shaped Reformed Protestantism across Europe and North America

cards.subject: ap-world-history
cards.prompt: The Tudor king of England whose break with Rome over his divorce from Catherine of Aragon created the Church of England and seized the wealth of English monasteries

cards.subject: ap-world-history
cards.prompt: The Tudor queen (r. 1558–1603) who consolidated Protestant Anglicanism, defeated the Spanish Armada, and presided over England's cultural golden age

cards.subject: ap-world-history
cards.prompt: The 15th-century German craftsman whose moveable-type printing press mass-produced books and spread Reformation pamphlets across Europe with unprecedented speed

cards.subject: ap-world-history
cards.prompt: The Spanish soldier-turned-mystic who founded the Society of Jesus in 1540 and wrote the Spiritual Exercises, leading the Counter-Reformation's educational and missionary drive
